  "findings": [
    "Sling angle is under 30 degrees, causing extremely high tension forces on the rigging components."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the lift immediately.",
    "Reconfigure the rigging setup using longer slings or a spreader bar to increase the horizontal sling angle to at least 45 degrees, preferably 60 degrees."
  ],
  "rationale": "Sling angles below 30 degrees are extremely hazardous and generally prohibited by safety standards because the tension in the sling legs multiplies rapidly, exceeding the rated capacity of the rigging hardware and risking catastrophic failure.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251(c)"
  ]
